title: lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. brownii
intro: Lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. brownii, also known as the Brown's pea or few-flowered peavine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and may also help to relax muscles and improve cardiovascular health. Traditionally, Lathyrus pauciflorus subsp. brownii is prepared as a tea or infusion, and may also be used in tincture or capsule form as a dietary supplement.
